mechanism_narrative: The Federal Reserve has cut FEDFUNDS from 5.25% in mid-2007 to 4.24% by December 2007, a sustained easing trajectory. Falling policy rates compress the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and lower borrowing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as the easing cycle continues.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS fell from 5.25 to 4.24 between mid-2007 and Dec-2007
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or mean forward return is not significantly positive (p>0.05) during easing months.

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month average net change in PAYEMS falls below +50,000 jobs, it signals a stalling labor market that historically precedes broader growth deceleration. This labor deterioration signal, once triggered, predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as earnings growth expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average net change in PAYEMS falls below +50,000
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS level 138495 thousand as of Dec-2007 with decelerating monthly gains
failure_condition: In periods where the PAYEMS trigger fires, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST (housing starts, already down sharply from ~2000k to 1187k units) combined with flattening RSAFS (retail sales) indicates a broad consumer and residential demand contraction that is not visible from either series alone. This joint weakening predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as consumption and housing-linked sectors reprice lower.
candidate_trigger: HOUST year-over-year decline exceeds 20% while RSAFS month-over-month growth turns negative
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1187k units in Nov-2007, down substantially from prior-year peak
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS conditions jointly trigger, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the joint-signal Rank IC is not materially higher than the single-series IC of either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a deceleration in INDPRO growth below its trailing 12-month average signals waning industrial momentum ahead of a broader slowdown. This supply-side deceleration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as forward earnings for cyclical and industrial sectors are marked down.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth rate falls below its trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.9 in Nov-2007 near cycle highs but growth rate slowing
failure_condition: During INDPRO deceleration episodes occurring in confirmed late-cycle regimes, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS has been cut by more than 100 basis points cumulatively over the trailing 6 months, it reveals the Fed is responding to acute growth or financial stress rather than routine normalization. This aggressive easing signal, rather than being reassuring, predicts short-term negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as markets price in the underlying deterioration the Fed is reacting to.
candidate_trigger: cumulative FEDFUNDS decline exceeds 100 basis points over trailing 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS cut from 5.25 to 4.24 over roughly 6 months into Dec-2007
failure_condition: Following triggered episodes of >100bp cumulative easing, forward 5d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the signal shows no statistically significant negative mean return (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in UNRATE, even from low absolute levels such as 5.0%, reflects gradual labor market softening that leads equity risk premia higher. Rising unemployment trend predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as investors demand higher compensation for growth uncertainty.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% in Dec-2007, up from cycle lows near 4.4%
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE 3-month trend slope and forward 20d SPX return is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or mean returns during rising-UNRATE months are not significantly negative (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a consumer-driven expansion regime, positive month-over-month growth in RSAFS signals resilient household spending that supports near-term corporate revenue and equity prices. Under this regime, RSAFS strength predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 382928 million dollars as of Nov-2007
regime_hint: consumer-driven expansion with stable labor market
failure_condition: If the regime condition (stable UNRATE, positive RSAFS growth) holds but forward 5d SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 within the regime, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When both INDPRO growth decelerates and PAYEMS net job gains slow simultaneously, the joint signal captures a synchronized supply-and-labor-side slowdown that is a stronger recession precursor than either series alone. This dual deceleration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth below trailing 12-month average AND 3-month average PAYEMS net change below +75,000
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.9 and PAYEMS at 138495 thousand as of late 2007 with both growth rates slowing
regime_hint: synchronized industrial and labor slowdown preceding recession
failure_condition: When the joint INDPRO-PAYEMS trigger fires, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the joint-signal Rank IC does not exceed the best single-series IC by a meaningful margin (>0.02) over any 3-year rolling window.
